commits.message: Streamline 318: one experiment at a time
commits.body:
Three birds sat perched upon the wire,
But only one may sing in choir.
The rest await their turn to fly—
A summary table tells them why.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Mark experiment 6 as success, add conclusion
commits.body:
The paste command works! A triumph so sweet,
Clipboard through XPC, the journey complete.
We logged what we learned and what's left to do—
Phase 1 is done, Phase 2 waits in queue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add issue 318: Cmd keybindings for webview
commits.body:
The paste command works, but copy does not,
A journey half-traveled, the other half sought.
From 317's sprawl we extract what remains—
Cmd+C/X/A awaits us, with clipboard-shaped pains.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Implement clipboard paste via XPC proxy
commits.body:
The clipboard refused to cross the line,
Between two processes, yours and mine.
So GUI reads what users copy,
And XPC sends it, never sloppy.

Now execCommand does the deed,
Inserting text at lightning speed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Experiment five got keys to flow,
But clipboard said "You shall not go!"
The process walls stand firm and tall,
So now through XPC we'll send it all.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
performKeyEquivalent holds the key,
To make Cmd+V a KeyEvent be.
We'll add it to the special list,
So clipboard shortcuts won't be missed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The clipboard mystery solved at last—
Cmd+V to menus was being cast.
performKeyEquivalent said "NO",
So KeyEvents had nowhere to go.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The paste command travels far and wide,
Through XPC tunnels, side by side.
But somewhere in the dark it's lost—
We'll add some logs to count the cost.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The keys flow free, the bindings cease,
But clipboard hides behind the fleece.
We type and tab with merry glee—
Yet Cmd+V stays a mystery.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Skip keybindings in Browse mode without consuming
commits.body:
We tried to block, but blocked too well,
The keys all fell into a shell.
Now we step back with gentle grace—
Let KeyEvent find its rightful place.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The gate we blocked was far too keen,
It swallowed keys, left nothing seen.
Now we simply step aside—
Let KeyEvent flow with the tide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Document experiment 2 failure: set_handled() kills KeyEvent
commits.body:
We blocked the gate with eager hand,
But starved the realm we meant to command.
set_handled() consumed the raw key whole—
No KeyEvent reached our webview's soul.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Design experiment 2: block keybindings in Browse mode
commits.body:
Two handlers lurk where keys must pass,
The raw one strikes before the glass.
Now we shall guard that early gate,
And make the browser's claim first-rate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Document experiment 1 success and keybinding issue
commits.body:
The keys now dance upon the page,
But paste sneaks through to terminal's cage.
Keybindings run before our gate—
Experiment 2 must set them straight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
At last the browser hears our call,
No longer deaf behind its wall.
Keys flow through XPC to CEF's ear,
Type "hello world"—the text appears!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The keys once fell into the void,
Their purpose utterly destroyed.
Now through XPC they shall flow,
To CEF where characters grow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add issue 317: input forwarding to webview
commits.body:
The webview sits pretty, a window to the world,
But silent it remains, no input unfurled.
Now we plan to teach it to hear every key,
So users can browse as they're meant to be free.
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Complete unified HSB dimming for webview
commits.body:
The panes now dim in harmony,
HSB from config flows.
Browse mode dims the panel see,
Control mode—webview goes.

Inactive? Both shall fade as one,
Like terminals beside.
The quad we caught, the work is done,
No brightness left to hide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A quad was born, then cast away,
Its HSV left unset.
Now we shall catch it, make it stay,
And dim it—don't forget!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The webview dimmed just fine,
But the panel stayed bright.
Two rendering paths entwined—
Background ignored the light.

One path drew the solid fill,
The other drew the text.
The first discarded quads at will,
HSV? What comes next?

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The shader had functions tucked away,
RGB to HSV, then back to play.
Why write them twice when once will do?
We'll borrow what WezTerm already knew.
Panel and browser, dim as one—
Config-driven, matching, done.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A config waits in WezTerm's keep:
Hue, saturation, brightness deep.
Why hardcode fifty percent dim,
When users set their own display whim?
Now panel, browser, pane alike
Shall fade in sync—a visual strike.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Ten checkboxes, all filled in,
Three experiments—a triple win.
Keys now blocked from terminal's ear,
Ctrl+C and Enter steer.
The panel speaks of mode and state,
While dimming shows the browser's fate.
Issue 315, at last, is done—
Browse and Control modes, as one.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Implement visual dimming in Control mode
commits.body:
The shader learned a brand new trick:
A uniform makes brightness flick.
In Browse mode, colors shine full bright,
But Control mode dims them—half the light.
Fifty percent, the pixels fade,
A visual cue that focus strayed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
When Control mode takes the stage,
The webview dims to show its cage.
A uniform whispers to the shader bright:
"Reduce your glow, withhold your light."
Half the brightness, half the gleam—
A darkened browser, as if in dream.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Three checkboxes, filled with care,
The panel shifts its text with flair.
Browse mode whispers where you've been,
Control mode tells you what keys mean.
The experiment has reached its end—
On to dimming, my old friend.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Show mode-aware text in control panel
commits.body:
The panel spoke but one refrain,
A URL, again, again.
Now Browse mode shows the page you're on,
While Control mode guides you along—
"Press Enter, or Ctrl+C to flee,"
The text adapts accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Implement mode switching and key interception
commits.body:
Two modes now guard the browser's gate:
Browse consumes, Control deliberates.
Ctrl+C toggles between the two,
While Enter returns to browsing view.

No keystroke slips to shell below,
The terminal's protected so.
From Browse to Control, then exit clean—
The smoothest mode switch ever seen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Before the keys can reach the shell,
A guardian checks—does webview dwell?
In Browse mode, all input stays,
In Control mode, bindings find their ways.

Ctrl+C toggles, Enter returns,
While terminal beneath unlearns
That any keystroke came its way—
The webview claims them all today.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two modes dance within the pane,
Browse and Control, a shifting reign.
Ctrl+C toggles, Enter returns,
While keys are caught before they burn.

No input shall the terminal see,
While browser holds the focus key.
A dimmed veil marks Control's embrace,
Till Enter brings Browse back in place.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Four experiments, a journey complete—
From URL pipes to rendering's feat.
Phase one now stands, the panel shines bright,
With background solid and text just right.

What comes next? The modes await,
Control and Browse to navigate.
Input forwarding, keys and clicks,
Then polish with UX tricks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Implement control panel with solid background
commits.body:
Two phases dance in render's flow:
First background, then text must show.
While layers live, the rectangle's drawn,
After they drop, the URL is spawned.

No more does terminal bleed through—
The control bar stands, opaque and true.
Phase one complete, the panel shines,
With URL displayed in proper lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A rectangle filled, opaque and true,
While layers live, before they're through.
Phase one for background, phase two for text—
Two steps in sequence, properly vexed.
The terminal bleeds through no more,
Once filled_rectangle paints the floor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The text appeared, crisp and clear,
But background? Not there, I fear.
Two phases ts2 employs—
One for background, one for poise.
We built just half; now we know why
The terminal peeks through from behind.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The text was rendered, but nobody saw—
Called after submission, a timing flaw.
Now paint_pass shall host the call,
After layers drop, before modal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Part A stood tall, the viewport true,
Two cells of space with a lovely view.
But Part B stumbled, text unseen—
Rendered too late, behind the screen.

The buffers flew before the call,
render_element missed it all.
To paint_pass we must relocate,
Before the GPU seals its fate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two cells high, the bar must stand,
A viewport shift by careful hand.
Part A adjusts where pixels land—
Push webview down as we have planned.

Part B shall paint the URL bright,
With margins half a cell in height.
But first the space must come to light,
Before the text can join the fight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The URL once lived in CEF's domain,
Locked away where none could claim.
But DisplayHandler heard the call—
"on_address_change!" it cried to all.

Through XPC the string now flies,
From profile server to GUI's eyes.
Each texture brings the page's name,
So control panels can stake their claim.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The URL must travel far and wide,
From profile server to the GUI side.
Through XPC it shall be sent,
With every texture, every event.

Option A we chose to take—
Dynamic updates for navigation's sake.
When pages change, the bar will know,
And proudly let the new URL show.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A bar atop the browser waits,
Two cells high to show its state.
Half-cell margins, left and top,
Where the URL shall never stop.

Phase one renders, nothing more—
No keys to press, no modes to store.
Just paint the bar, display the link,
And let the webview start to shrink.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A binary once lived in MacOS,
But focus it stole—that's how it goes.
We wrapped it in .app with plist decree:
"LSUIElement, set yourself free!"

Five parents we climbed to find Contents root,
The helper flag true, the framework resolute.
No dock icon glows, no focus is lost—
Declarative peace, well worth the cost.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Three tries to move a file,
Each path wrong in its own style.
Two levels? Five? Who can say?
The helper found its home today.
Now LSUIElement shines bright,
No dock icon steals the light.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Five levels up, not merely two,
To find the helpers, tried and true.
The GPU awaits its proper guide—
Let's see if this turns the tide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The framework loaded, we cheered with glee,
But helpers hid where they shouldn't be.
Two levels up was not enough—
The path math keeps getting tough.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The framework hides, the loader seeks,
But "false" led down the wrong techniques.
One param flipped from false to true—
Will guide the loader right on through.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The helper app was bundled right,
But CEF got lost without its light.
The path resolver said "false" not "true"—
Now we know just what to do.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The binary sits in MacOS bare,
But Apple wants a bundle there.
We'll wrap it up with Info.plist,
LSUIElement—the proper twist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A binary loose in MacOS/,
Not quite the way that Apple knows.
We'll wrap it in a helper's guise,
With Info.plist—the proper prize.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A focus thief once prowled the dock,
Now banished by a policy lock.
We traced the crime, we found the cure—
Our terminal's focus is secure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The thief is caught, the case is closed,
Hypothesis A: confirmed, exposed.
No dock icon, no stolen keys—
Our terminal types with quiet ease.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles
